commit.message:
fix(circuit-breaker): add reset_circuit_breakers bundle field for manual reset (#21)

When auto_reset_after_minutes=0, there was no way to reset a tripped
circuit breaker without restarting nginx workers. This adds an optional
reset_circuit_breakers field to the policy bundle: when present, apply()
calls circuit_breaker.reset() for each listed limit_key before activating
the new bundle.

Deliberately requires a new bundle deploy (not a direct admin API) so
the free/open-core path has a working but inconvenient reset mechanism.
A SaaS control plane can make this one-click by issuing a bundle with
the reset field pre-populated.

Closes #17

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message: ITE-3295 remove unused jonasland helpers (#1177) ## Summary - remove unused helper exports from `packages/shared/src/jonasland/index.ts` - delete the support types and imports that only existed for those dead helpers - keep the shared RPC client helper surface used elsewhere in the repo intact ## Validation - `pnpm exec tsc -p packages/shared/tsconfig.json --noEmit` - `pnpm dlx ts-prune -p packages/shared/tsconfig.json | rg ...` => `no-targets-reported` - `git diff --check HEAD^ HEAD` --- --- > [!NOTE] > **Low Risk** > Removes dead exported helpers and related types/imports without changing the remaining tracing/logging/RPC client behavior. Main risk is downstream breakage if any external consumer still imports the deleted exports. > > **Overview** > Prunes `packages/shared/src/jonasland/index.ts` by deleting several unused exported helpers and their supporting types/imports. > > Removed surface includes the service sub-router handler builder, ORPC context middleware (and `randomUUID` usage), the Vite browser error bridge plugin, and the OpenAPI/WebSocket ORPC client helpers plus related URL resolvers/types; the existing fetch-based `createOrpcRpcServiceClient` API remains. > > Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 062dea9068756cd490b9b532766e7eaaee42b4e9. commit.message:
Cover missing inquirer edge cases

Add regression-style tests for the prompt validation paths that were
still missing in @optique/inquirer, including password and editor
validation failures.

Expand number prompt coverage to include negative values, boundary
values, and decimal-friendly configuration, and add normalization
checks for empty, separator-only, and metadata-heavy choice lists.

Document the current primitive-state annotation behavior with an
explicit test so future refactors have a locked expectation.

Closes https://github.com/dahlia/optique/issues/167

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

- show remaining generation quota in the top bar and on the questionnaire generate screen

commit.message:
Use file paths for inquirer JSDoc fixture reads

Convert the inquirer JSDoc regression tests to resolve the source
file to a path string before calling readFileSync(). This avoids
relying on URL support details in node-compat layers across Deno,
Node.js, and Bun.

https://github.com/dahlia/optique/pull/171#discussion_r2929633489

node-compat differences around URL handling.

commit.message:
Anchor inquirer JSDoc extraction to declarations

Make the inquirer JSDoc regression helper locate the target
declaration first and then read only the adjacent preceding
doc block. This removes over-capture from earlier comments and
keeps the matcher portable across LF and CRLF checkouts.

https://github.com/dahlia/optique/pull/171#discussion_r2929597506
https://github.com/dahlia/optique/pull/171#discussion_r2929603391

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Anchor env JSDoc extraction to declarations

Make the env JSDoc regression helper find the target declaration
first and then read only the adjacent preceding doc block. This
avoids false positives from earlier comments and tolerates CRLF
line endings via trailing-whitespace matching.

https://github.com/dahlia/optique/pull/171#discussion_r2929597485
https://github.com/dahlia/optique/pull/171#discussion_r2929603389

commit.message:
검토 도메인 액션 API 및 권한 스켈레톤 추가 (#4)

* 검토 도메인 엔티티 추가

* 검토 추가 검토자 모델과 에러코드 추가

* 검토 임시 권한 컨텍스트와 포트 추가

* 검토 액션 API 스켈레톤과 DTO 추가

* 검토 액션 서비스를 유스케이스별로 분리

* 검토 액션 정책 테스트 추가

* 검토 업무 버전 필드 제거

* 검토 명령 서비스를 단일 서비스로 단순화

* feat: add korean swagger docs for review APIs

* fix(Review)Entity 불변식 검증 추가

private 생성자 추가
생성자 컨벤션 맞춤

* refactor(review): separate service contracts from controller dto

* refactor(review): remove lock version from additional reviewer api

* refactor(review): Task 연관 대신 taskId를 사용하도록 변경

- Review 엔티티가 Task 연관 대신 taskId만 보관하도록 수정\n- Review 저장소와 서비스가 taskId 기준으로 조회하도록 정리\n- 커밋과 PR 메시지 한글 작성 규칙 문서 추가

* fix(review): 반려 사유 입력과 도메인 검증을 함께 적용

- 반려 요청 DTO에 길이 제한 검증 추가\n- Review 엔티티에 반려 사유 최종 검증 추가\n- 내부 검증 실패를 VALIDATION_ERROR로 응답하도록 예외 처리 추가

commit.message:
feat(hardware): clarify script requirements and loopback knobs

Summary:
- add explicit `## Hardware Requirements` sections to the remaining
  maintained hardware scripts under `test/integration/hardware/scripts`
- document bench-specific adaptation points in the harder scripts so
  maintained topology assumptions are visible in the file itself
- add bounded `--input-channel` and `--output-channel` flags to
  `dc_sync.exs` for the jitter comparison phase

Rationale:
- the scripts were useful but still assumed too much bench context,
  especially around required slaves, wiring, and where to adapt them
- the DC jitter path was still hardcoded to `ch1`, which made the
  maintained loopback assumption harder to reuse on a slightly different
  bench without editing the script first

Validation:
- mix format test/integration/hardware/scripts/bench.exs
  test/integration/hardware/scripts/dc_sync.exs
  test/integration/hardware/scripts/diag.exs
  test/integration/hardware/scripts/fault_tolerance.exs
  test/integration/hardware/scripts/multi_domain.exs
  test/integration/hardware/scripts/probe.exs
  test/integration/hardware/scripts/rtd_stability.exs
  test/integration/hardware/scripts/scan.exs
  test/integration/hardware/scripts/sdo_debug.exs
  test/integration/hardware/scripts/udp_transport.exs
  test/integration/hardware/scripts/watchdog_recovery.exs
  test/integration/hardware/scripts/wiring_map.exs
- MIX_ENV=test mix run -e 'for path <- ~w(test/integration/hardware/
  scripts/bench.exs test/integration/hardware/scripts/dc_sync.exs
  test/integration/hardware/scripts/diag.exs
  test/integration/hardware/scripts/fault_tolerance.exs
  test/integration/hardware/scripts/multi_domain.exs
  test/integration/hardware/scripts/probe.exs
  test/integration/hardware/scripts/rtd_stability.exs
  test/integration/hardware/scripts/scan.exs
  test/integration/hardware/scripts/sdo_debug.exs
  test/integration/hardware/scripts/udp_transport.exs
  test/integration/hardware/scripts/watchdog_recovery.exs
  test/integration/hardware/scripts/wiring_map.exs), do:
  Code.string_to_quoted!(File.read!(path), file: path)'
- git diff --check -- test/integration/hardware/scripts

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(skill): include droid platform guidance

Update skill CLI platform_config validation guidance to list droid keys alongside the existing schema-backed platform entries, and add regression coverage for invalid droid keys.

Task: @01KKJKM61M5BAEFRPNBRK7XDRY

Spec: @droid-platform-config

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(cli): restore and harden session/settings migration (#27)

* fix(settings): support nested env/defaults structure and new env var names

- Rename settings file from setting.json to settings.json (with legacy fallback)
- Support nested structure with 'env' and 'defaults' sections
- Add support for AGENTLET_API_KEY and AGENTLET_BASE_URL env vars
- Maintain backward compatibility with flat structure and OPENAI_* env vars
- Add comprehensive tests for new functionality
- Extract shared helpers for validation and settings construction
- Add change detection for os.environ mutations

Fixes settings loading to work with user's ~/.agentlet/settings.json format.

* test: add explicit monkeypatch cleanup for env vars in settings tests

Add monkeypatch.delenv() calls before load_settings() to ensure proper
cleanup of environment variables set during tests. This makes the test
isolation explicit and prevents potential pollution of the global env state.

* refactor(settings): remove env section support from settings.json

- Remove _apply_env_section() function that loaded env vars from settings
- Simplify _load_nested_settings() to only handle defaults section
- Update load_settings() to detect nested structure by 'defaults' key only
- Remove tests for env section functionality
- Remove non-existent inject_project_env import from test script

BREAKING CHANGE: The 'env' section in settings.json is no longer supported.
Environment variables should be set externally (e.g., in shell profile).

* refactor(settings): clarify docstring for load_settings

Remove the confusing mention of 'env' section no longer being
supported since this feature was never implemented.

* feat(settings): add LiteLLM-standard provider-specific env var resolution

- Add provider-to-env mappings for API keys and base URLs following
  LiteLLM naming conventions (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, AZURE_API_KEY, etc.)
- Unify _resolve_api_key and _resolve_api_base into single
  _resolve_provider_env function with keyword parameters
- Normalize provider name once to avoid redundant .lower() calls
- Add comprehensive tests for provider-specific env var resolution
- Maintain backward compatibility with OPENAI_API_KEY as fallback

Priority order:
1. AGENTLET_API_KEY (project-specific override)
2. {PROVIDER}_API_KEY (LiteLLM standard)
3. OPENAI_API_KEY (backward compatibility)
4. settings.json value

* chore: add AI coding agent directories to .gitignore

* refactor(sessions): migrate session storage to ~/.agentlet

Migrate session and history storage from {cwd}/.agentlet/ to ~/.agentlet/
to make agentlet behave more like a standard Python tool.

Changes:
- Sessions stored at ~/.agentlet/sessions/{cwd_hash}/
- History stored globally at ~/.agentlet/history
- Added get_data_dir() and _cwd_hash() helper functions
- Updated SessionStore to accept optional data_dir parameter
- Added pytest fixture to reduce test boilerplate
- Updated documentation for new storage paths

The cwd_hash is the first 16 chars of MD5 hash of the resolved cwd path,
providing isolation between projects while keeping data centralized.

* docs: add comprehensive CLI usage guide and update README

- Complete rewrite of README with quick start, configuration, and CLI usage
- New docs/cli-usage.md with detailed guide covering:
  - Settings file and environment variable configuration
  - Provider-specific setup for OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek, etc.
  - Command reference for init and chat commands
  - One-shot vs interactive mode examples
  - Session management documentation
  - Troubleshooting guide
- Updated design doc with configuration management section
- Added documentation section to README with links to all guides

* chore: update default model from gpt-4o-mini to gpt-5.4

Update all references to use gpt-5.4 as the default model:
- README.md examples and configuration table
- docs/cli-usage.md examples and settings
- Design docs default references
- DEFAULT_MODEL constant in registry.py
- Test fixtures and assertions

* fix(provider): support documented LiteLLM backends

Summary:
- register all documented LiteLLM-backed provider names in the runtime
- align real API helpers with exported env vars and ~/.agentlet/settings.json
- add registry coverage for supported provider names and unknown-provider errors

Rationale:
- the docs now advertise non-OpenAI providers, so the runtime must accept
  those provider names instead of failing at chat startup
- the real API helpers should reflect the current configuration model rather
  than referring to removed .env loading behavior

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_provider_registry.py
  tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py tests/unit/test_cli_settings.py
  tests/smoke/test_cli_chat.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(cli): restore session and settings migration paths

Summary:
- add fallback and migration logic for legacy project-local sessions
- allow `--session` to load transcripts across cwd hash buckets
- write future config updates to the canonical settings.json path

Rationale:
- preserve resume behavior for users upgrading from the old storage layout
- make explicit session IDs work with the new home-scoped session store
- complete the settings filename migration without breaking legacy reads

Tests:
- uv run pytest

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* refactor(cli): remove --session flags and CLI env var overrides

Remove the ability to pass --session, --continue, --new-session flags
and --provider, --model, --api-key, --api-base, --temperature,
--max-tokens overrides via CLI. Configuration now comes only from
~/.agentlet/settings.json or environment variables.

Changes:
- Remove session resume functionality from chat command
- Remove CLI argument overrides for provider settings
- Simplify chat_app.py by removing load_session_for_resume()
- Add _create_provider_config() helper for type-safe config creation
- Move SessionStore creation inside interactive mode block
- Update tests to reflect simplified CLI interface
- Update documentation in README.md and cli-usage.md

This simplifies the CLI surface and establishes a clearer
configuration hierarchy: env vars > settings file > defaults.

* fix(cli): restore chat resume and override flags

Summary:
- restore `chat` parser support for session resume flags and per-call
  provider settings overrides
- wire interactive resume back through the home-scoped session store
  and show resumed session state in the REPL header
- add regression coverage for parser acceptance, resumed sessions, and
  one-shot override propagation

Rationale:
- preserve the existing CLI contract expected by users, tests, and the
  updated documentation
- keep the new `~/.agentlet` migration path reachable from the actual
  CLI instead of leaving it stranded behind removed flags

Tests:
- uv run pytest

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* refactor(cli): remove session resume flags and CLI env var overrides

- Remove --continue, --session, --new-session flags from chat command
- Remove AGENTLET_API_KEY, AGENTLET_BASE_URL, AGENTLET_PROVIDER, AGENTLET_MODEL env vars
- Simplify settings to only use settings.json and built-in defaults
- Prevent stale credentials when overriding provider via CLI
- Update all documentation to reflect new configuration approach
- Update tests to match new behavior

* fix(cli): harden session hashing and migration

Summary:
- replace MD5 cwd hashing with truncated SHA-256 session bucket hashes
- bound cross-bucket session lookup and warn on duplicate session ids
- make legacy migration writes atomic and type CLI setting overrides

Rationale:
- avoid weak hashing primitives in filesystem path generation
- keep session resolution deterministic when the same id appears twice
- prevent concurrent migration writers from overwriting each other

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_sessions.py tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
refactor(test): share support helpers across suites

Summary:
- move FakeBus into shared test/support and compile shared plus
  integration support paths in test
- replace duplicated inline fake bus modules with the shared helper
  and split domain health notification coverage into its own file
- remove fixed sleeps from the touched tests and use deterministic
  queue, trace, or eventual assertions instead

Rationale:
- reduce duplicated test doubles and stop unit-style tests from
  depending on an integration-only support path
- make the touched tests less timing-sensitive while preserving the
  behavior each case is meant to exercise
- keep the refactor honest by fixing the review findings around
  FakeBus reply parsing and bus telemetry scoping before commit

Validation:
- mix test

commit.message:
refactor(api): normalize queries and unify simulator helpers

Summary:
- wrap public EtherCAT query results in `{:ok, value}` and normalize
  `stop/0` to return `{:error, :already_stopped}` when absent
- update capture, simulator, hardware, and focused API callers to the
  tagged-tuple query shape
- collapse simulator ring variants into `SimulatorRing`, remove
  `Scenario.expect_eventually/4`, and document/parameterize the first
  maintained hardware scripts more clearly

Rationale:
- query functions now follow a cleaner Elixir API shape without changing
  the lower-level `Master.API.safe_call/1` contract
- one ring builder surface is easier to maintain than parallel helper
  modules with copy-pasted topology and fixture defaults
- `Scenario` stays responsible for orchestration while `Expect` owns
  polling assertions, which makes the scenario DSL smaller and clearer

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat_test.exs test/ethercat/capture_test.exs
  test/integration/simulator --max-failures 1
- mix test test/integration/support/scenario_test.exs
  test/integration/simulator/36_el3202_reconnect_preop_event_disconnect_mix_test.exs
  --max-failures 1
- mix test test/integration/simulator --max-failures 1
- MIX_ENV=test mix run -e 'for path <- ["test/integration/hardware/scripts/cycle_jitter.exs", "test/integration/hardware/scripts/el3202.exs", "test/integration/hardware/scripts/loopback.exs"], do: Code.string_to_quoted!(File.read!(path), file: path)'

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Attach a Bun fs.watch error handler so deleted project roots do not surface as unhandled ENOENT failures during daemon session-sync teardown. Stop cleanly when the watched .kspec root disappears instead of scheduling recovery retries, and cover the deleted-root path with a focused regression test.

  module briefing to point at the new layout

commit.message:
chore(deps): refresh lockfile

Summary:
- update the locked usage_rules toolchain versions in mix.lock
- pick up newer igniter, rewrite, sourceror, spitfire, and
  usage_rules releases
- update the locked telemetry version in the local dev/test
  environment

Rationale:
- the lockfile had already moved forward in the local environment and
  should be committed separately from the usage_rules config fix
- keeping this as a standalone dependency commit avoids mixing it with
  unrelated source and test changes still in the worktree

Validation:
- not run

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(master): tighten dc recovery and config validation

Summary:
- keep the DC runtime fault latched until a restarted DC worker
  proves a successful maintenance tick
- fail startup on real DC initialization errors instead of silently
  degrading to no-DC mode
- reject duplicate domain ids and duplicate slave names during config
  normalization and add regressions for the recovery path

Rationale:
- the master should not report healthy OP state before DC runtime has
  actually recovered
- explicit DC configuration should remain a real startup contract
  rather than best-effort behavior
- duplicate config should fail early, before domains or slaves start
  under ambiguous identities

Validation:
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(repo): make usage_rules sync idempotent

Summary:
- switch AGENTS.md syncing to explicit usage_rules sub-rules with
  main disabled
- remove the duplicated generic usage_rules block from AGENTS.md
- drop the stale generated elixir-otp skill that no longer matches the
  config

Rationale:
- the built-in :elixir and :otp aliases also inline usage_rules.md,
  which caused mix usage_rules.sync to reintroduce duplicate sections
- using explicit sub-rules keeps AGENTS.md limited to the Elixir and
  OTP guidance this repo actually wants and makes sync stable

Validation:
- mix usage_rules.sync --yes
- mix usage_rules.sync --yes
- mix format mix.exs
- git diff --check -- mix.exs AGENTS.md .claude/skills/elixir-otp

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(dispatch): recover reviewer snapshot registry state

Persist stale registry records from metadata-backed reviewer snapshots when the
worker registration is missing so pending_review pruning reports an unhealthy
workspace instead of treating it as absent.

Task: @01KKJP6E1TN5ZFMHASTHSGG2MH
Spec: @dispatch-workspace-cleanup-policy
Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(sdk): expand Direct API coverage and align core contracts

Summary:
- add service support for ad images, agency clients, bid modifiers,
  dictionaries, keyword bids, keywords, and sitelinks
- align core Ads, Campaigns, and AdGroups behavior closer to the
  official API surface, including broader Ads payload support
- add explicit request apiVersion support for official v5 and v501
  service endpoints
- extend README and contract tests to cover the expanded SDK surface

Rationale:
- move the SDK from MVP coverage toward broader Yandex Direct API
  coverage while keeping the transport and error model consistent
- remove non-official AdGroups methods and reflect official endpoint
  versioning in the public request options

Tests:
- npm run build
- npm test

ci(repo): add GitHub Actions test and release workflows

Summary:
- add a minimal Linux CI workflow for format, compile, and test checks
- add a tag-driven release workflow that validates the tag version,
  builds docs and the Hex package, and creates a draft GitHub release
- update RELEASE.md to describe the new changelog and draft-release
  automation flow

Rationale:
- the repo needs a small default CI path that matches its current
  Linux-first library constraints without app or container complexity
- release automation should follow the documented Hex-library flow and
  fail early when the pushed tag does not match the package version
- the manual release checklist must stay aligned with the automated
  GitHub release draft behavior to avoid process drift

Validation:
- git diff --check
- local workflow files reviewed; GitHub Actions were not executed from
  this session

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(api): clarify root EtherCAT public contracts

Summary:
- add named public types for the root EtherCAT API surface, including
  session state, query errors, and slave/domain snapshots
- tighten root specs to use those aliases instead of loose map and
  list return types
- fix docs for bus nil semantics, state query errors, and structured
  slave configuration_error values

Rationale:
- the root module is the public entry point, so its contracts should be
  explicit and readable without chasing internal implementation files
- state query failures are wrapper-call errors, not lifecycle states,
  and the docs should make that distinction obvious
- inaccurate docs around configuration_error and bus nil behavior make
  the public API easier to misunderstand than it needs to be

Validation:
- mix format lib/ethercat.ex
- mix compile
- mix docs.fresh

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
